transformation_type: inversion-enabled substitution of alcohols using phosphine/azodicarboxylate reagents
mechanism_type: phosphine/azodicarboxylate activation of alcohols followed by nucleophilic displacement
common_conditions: mild phosphine/azodicarboxylate conditions; broad O/N/S/C nucleophile scope; inversion at secondary alcohols
description_short: Widely used substitution protocol that activates alcohols with phosphine and azo reagents to enable stereoinvertive displacement by diverse nucleophiles.

transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed borylation of aryl or vinyl electrophiles to boronic esters
mechanism_type: oxidative addition / diboron transmetalation / reductive elimination sequence
reactant_pattern_text: aryl or vinyl halide/triflate + diboron reagent
common_conditions: Pd catalysis with acetate base; useful for preparation of Suzuki-coupling partners
description_short: Palladium-catalyzed installation of boronic ester functionality from aryl or vinyl electrophiles, providing versatile organoboron coupling partners.

transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted aldol coupling of silyl enol ethers with carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: carbon-carbon bond formation between activated carbonyls and preformed enol silanes
common_conditions: Lewis acid mediated; anti/syn stereochemical control and catalytic asymmetric variants common
description_short: Powerful aldol variant using preformed enol silanes and Lewis acid activation to deliver beta-hydroxy carbonyl products with high stereocontrol.

transformation_type: diastereoselective alpha-alkylation of pseudoephedrine-derived amide enolates
mechanism_type: auxiliary-controlled enolate alkylation followed by auxiliary removal
common_conditions: pseudoephedrine auxiliary; high diastereoselectivity in alpha-alkylation of amide enolates
description_short: Chiral auxiliary-based alkylation method that enables highly selective construction of alpha-substituted carboxylic acid derivatives.

transformation_type: conjugate hydrocyanation of activated alkenes or enones
mechanism_type: Lewis-acid-activated 1,4-addition of cyanide to alpha,beta-unsaturated systems
reactant_pattern_text: enone or activated alkene + dialkylaluminum cyanide/HCN
common_conditions: dialkylaluminum cyanides or HCN/AlX3 combinations; hydrolytic workup yields beta-cyano carbonyls
description_short: Hydrocyanation protocol for conjugate cyanide addition to enones and related Michael acceptors using organoaluminum cyanide reagents.
